context.projectOverview: agent-bober TS/ESM CLI. Plan spec-20260605-scale-safe-history-memory. Sprint 3 adds a PURE deterministic distill(history, contracts) -> LessonEntry[] (structured fields only; NO LLM, NO network, NO Date.now in the pure fn) plus a `bober memory distill|list|show` CLI command. distill persists via Sprint 2's appendLesson, deduped by content hash; idempotent. The CLI is the ONLY entry point — nothing in pipeline.ts triggers distill.

completedSprints.title: Bounded history reads + crash-safe rotation
completedSprints.summary: src/state/history.ts: loadHistory (reads archive+active concat, full stream), loadRecentHistory tail reader; src/state/history-rotation.ts rotation. history.maxActiveLines zod field. Use loadHistory to read full history in distill.

completedSprints.title: Deterministic lessons memory store
completedSprints.summary: src/state/memory.ts: LessonEntrySchema (mandatory non-empty sourceEntryRefs), appendLesson(root,lesson) writes .bober/memory/<id>.md + upserts one INDEX.md line, loadLessonIndex(root,{limit}) index-only, loadLesson(root,id) round-trip. NOT barrel-exported in src/state/index.ts — import directly from ../../state/memory.js. severity enum ['info','warn','high'].

contract.title: Deterministic distillation + bober memory CLI (distill | list | show)
contract.description: Add a pure, deterministic distill function that compresses sprint history plus eval outcomes into provenance-tagged LessonEntry records using only structured fields (recurring failed-criterion categories, repeated failing eval strategies, sprints exceeding an iteration threshold) — no LLM, no network call. Expose it through a new `bober memory distill | list | show` command. Distillation is idempotent: re-running adds no duplicate lessons. The command is the only entry point; nothing in pipeline.ts triggers it.

successCriteria.description: A pure distill(historyEntries, contracts) in src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts returns a deterministic LessonEntry[] derived only from structured fields; a test over a fixed fixture asserts an exact expected lesson set, stable across repeated calls.

distill plus appendLesson is idempotent: running twice over identical input yields an INDEX.md with no duplicate lessonId, deduped by a content hash of category+tags+sourceEntryRefs; a test asserts the second run adds zero new index lines.
The distill path constructs no LLM provider client and performs no network call; a test asserts via a guard or spy that no provider is instantiated during distill().

`bober memory distill` reads full history via loadHistory plus contracts, writes lessons, and prints a count summary; `bober memory list` prints the bounded index; `bober memory show <lessonId>` prints one lesson with its sourceEntryRefs. A test invokes the handlers against a temp project and asserts the printed counts and that show renders provenance.
npm run typecheck and npm run build exit 0 and the memory/distill and CLI tests pass with no new failures beyond the documented flaky baseline.

nonGoals: Do not use an LLM, embeddings, or any network call to produce or rank lessons.
Do not trigger distillation from pipeline.ts or any autopilot path — the CLI command is the only entry point.
Do not wire the planner to read lessons yet — that is sprint 4.
Do not change loadHistory or the LessonEntry schema from sprint 2.
stopConditions: Stop when distill is deterministic and idempotent, asserts zero LLM and network usage, and the three bober memory subcommands run against a temp project with passing tests.
Stop when changes are confined to src/orchestrator/memory/, src/cli/commands/memory.ts, the CLI command registration site, and collocated tests.
contract.definitionOfDone: Running `bober memory distill` compresses sprint history into deterministic, provenance-tagged lessons with no LLM or network call, idempotently so a re-run adds no duplicates. `bober memory list` and `bober memory show` let a human inspect the store. The distill function is pure and unit-tested against a fixture, and typecheck, build, and tests pass.
assumptions: distill can read the full history via the existing loadHistory and the contracts via listContracts; sprint 1 hardening is not a prerequisite for correctness, only for scale.
A content hash over category+tags+sourceEntryRefs is a stable dedupe key for idempotency.
contract.generatorNotes: Create src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts exporting a pure `distill(history: HistoryEntry[], contracts: SprintContract[]): LessonEntry[]`. Derive lessons only from structured data: group eval failures by criterion category / verificationMethod, count recurrences, flag sprints whose iterationHistory length exceeds a threshold (e.g. >= evaluator.maxIterations). Each LessonEntry: deterministic lessonId from the content hash (no Date.now in the pure function — the CLI stamps createdAt when persisting, or pass a clock in), category, tags from the failure signature, summary from a fixed template, occurrences = count, severity by count band, sourceEntryRefs = the history entry identifiers / sprintIds the lesson aggregates. Keep distill side-effect-free; persistence happens in the command. Create src/cli/commands/memory.ts with subcommands distill|list|show mirroring sprint.ts structure; distill calls loadHistory + listContracts, runs distill(), persists via appendLesson (dedupe by hash), prints `distilled N lessons (M new)`. Register the command where other CLI commands register (mirror sprint registration). Add a test guard ensuring no provider import is exercised in distill(). Use import type; ESM .js specifiers. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-3): deterministic distillation + bober memory CLI.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Verify distill() returns the same LessonEntry set across repeated calls on a fixed fixture and that lessons reference only structured fields. Verify idempotency: distill+persist twice, assert INDEX.md unchanged on the second run. Verify the no-LLM/no-network guard fires (assert no provider client constructed). Invoke the three subcommands against a temp project seeded with a history fixture: distill prints the count summary and writes lessons; list prints the bounded index; show <id> renders one lesson including sourceEntryRefs. Run npm run typecheck (exit 0), npm run build (exit 0), and the new tests. Confirm git diff is confined to src/orchestrator/memory/, src/cli/commands/memory.ts, the registration site, and tests; confirm pipeline.ts has no new call into distill.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
